 21â†’ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.

 23â†’public class CommentWrappingRule :

 36â†’    ) {
 37â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39â†’                node
 40â†’                    .prevLeaf()
 41â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43â†’                node
 44â†’                    .nextLeaf()
 45â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }

 48â†’                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null

 52â†’                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53â†’                    //    some comment
 54â†’                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55â†’                    emit(
 56â†’                        node.startOffset,
 57â†’                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58â†’                            "disallowed",
 59â†’                        false,
 60â†’                    )
 61â†’                } else {
 62â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63â†’                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64â†’                    emit(

 86â†’/**
 87â†’ * This rule inserts missing newlines (e.g. between parentheses of a multi-line function call). This logic previously
 88â†’ * was part of the IndentationRule (phase 1).
 89â†’ *
 90â†’ * Whenever a linebreak is inserted, this rules assumes that the parent node it indented correctly. So the indentation
 91â†’ * is fixed with respect to indentation of the parent. This is just a simple best effort for the case that the
 92â†’ * indentation rule is not run.
 93â†’ */
 94â†’public class WrappingRule :
 95â†’    StandardRule(
 96â†’        id = "wrapping",
 97â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 98â†’            setOf(
 99â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
100â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
101â†’                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
102â†’            ),
103â†’        visitorModifiers =
104â†’            setOf(
105â†’                RunAfterRule(
106â†’                    ruleId = ANNOTATION_RULE_ID,
107â†’                    mode = REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED,
108â†’                ),
109â†’            ),
110â†’    ) {
111â†’    private var line = 1
112â†’    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
113â†’    private var maxLineLength: Int = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
114â†’
115â†’    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
116â†’        line = 1
117â†’        indentConfig = IndentConfig(
118â†’            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
119â†’            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
120â†’        )
121â†’        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
122â†’    }
123â†’
124â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
125â†’        node: ASTNode,
126â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
127â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
128â†’    ) {
129â†’        when (node.elementType) {
130â†’            BLOCK -> beforeVisitBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit)
131â†’            LPAR, LBRACKET -> rearrangeBlock(node, autoCorrect, emit) // TODO: LT
132â†’            SUPER_TYPE_LIST -> rearrangeSuperTypeList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
133â†’            VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST, VALUE_ARGUMENT_LIST -> rearrangeValueList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
134â†’            TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST, TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeArgumentList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
135â†’//            TYPE_PARAMETER_LIST -> rearrangeTypeParameterList(node, autoCorrect, emit)
136â†’            ARROW -> rearrangeArrow(node, autoCorrect, emit)
137â†’            WHITE_SPACE -> line += node.text.count { it == '\n' }
138â†’            CLOSING_QUOTE -> rearrangeClosingQuote(node, autoCorrect, emit)
139â†’        }
140â†’    }

142â†’    private fun beforeVisitBlock(
143â†’        node: ASTNode,
144â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
145â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
146â†’    ) {
147â†’        require(node.elementType == BLOCK)
148â†’
149â†’        val startOfBlock = node.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
150â†’        if (startOfBlock?.elementType != LBRACE) {
151â†’            return
152â†’        }
153â†’        val blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline = startOfBlock.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
154â†’        val endOfBlock = node.lastChildLeafOrSelf().nextLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() && !it.isWhiteSpace() }
155â†’        val blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline = endOfBlock?.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
156â†’        val wrapBlock =
157â†’            when {
158â†’                startOfBlock.isPartOf(LONG_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY) -> {
159â†’                    // String template inside raw string literal may exceed the maximum line length
160â†’                    false
161â†’                }
162â†’                blockIsPrecededByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> false
163â†’                node.textContains('\n') || blockIsFollowedByWhitespaceContainingNewline -> {
164â†’                    // A multiline block should always be wrapped unless it starts with an EOL comment
165â†’                    node.firstChildLeafOrSelf().elementType != EOL_COMMENT
166â†’                }
167â†’                maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF -> {
168â†’                    val lengthUntilBeginOfLine =
169â†’                        node
170â†’                            .leaves(false)
171â†’                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
172â†’                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
173â†’                    val lengthUntilEndOfLine =
174â†’                        node
175â†’                            .firstChildLeafOrSelf()
176â†’                            .leavesIncludingSelf()
177â†’                            .takeWhile { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
178â†’                            .sumOf { it.textLength }
179â†’                    lengthUntilBeginOfLine + lengthUntilEndOfLine > maxLineLength
180â†’                }
181â†’                else -> false
182â†’            }
183â†’        if (wrapBlock) {
184â†’            startOfBlock
185â†’                .takeIf { !it.nextLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
186â†’                ?.let { leafNodeBeforeBlock ->
187â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
188â†’                        leafNodeBeforeBlock,
189â†’                        autoCorrect,
190â†’                        emit,
191â†’                    )
192â†’                }
193â†’        }
194â†’    }

196â†’    private fun rearrangeBlock(
197â†’        node: ASTNode,
198â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
199â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
200â†’    ) {
201â†’        val rElementType = MATCHING_RTOKEN_MAP[node.elementType]
202â†’        var newlineInBetween = false
203â†’        var parameterListInBetween = false
204â†’        var numberOfArgs = 0
205â†’        var firstArg: ASTNode? = null
206â†’        // matching ), ] or }
207â†’        val r = node.nextSibling {
208â†’            val isValueArgument = it.elementType == VALUE_ARGUMENT
209â†’            val hasLineBreak = if (isValueArgument) it.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN) else it.hasLineBreak()
210â†’            newlineInBetween = newlineInBetween || hasLineBreak
211â†’            parameterListInBetween = parameterListInBetween || it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST
212â†’            if (isValueArgument) {
213â†’                numberOfArgs++
214â†’                firstArg = it
215â†’            }
216â†’            it.elementType == rElementType
217â†’        }!!
218â†’        if (
219â†’            !newlineInBetween ||
220â†’            // keep { p ->
221â†’            // }
222â†’            (node.elementType == LBRACE && parameterListInBetween) ||
223â†’            // keep ({
224â†’            // }) and (object : C {
225â†’            // })
226â†’            (
227â†’                numberOfArgs == 1 &&
228â†’                    firstArg?.firstChildNode?.elementType
229â†’                        ?.let { it == OBJECT_LITERAL || it == LAMBDA_EXPRESSION } == true
230â†’                )
231â†’        ) {
232â†’            return
233â†’        }
234â†’        if (node.isPartOfForLoopConditionWithMultilineExpression()) {
235â†’            // keep:
236â†’            // for (foo in listOf(
237â†’            //     "foo-1",
238â†’            //     "foo-2"
239â†’            // )) { ... }
240â†’            // but reject:
241â†’            // for (
242â†’            //     foo in listOf(
243â†’            //         "foo-1",
244â†’            //         "foo-2"
245â†’            //     )
246â†’            // ) { ... }
247â†’            return
248â†’        }
249â†’        if (!node.nextCodeLeaf()?.prevLeaf {
250â†’                // Skip comments, whitespace, and empty nodes
251â†’                !it.isPartOfComment() &&
252â†’                    !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() &&
253â†’                    it.textLength > 0
254â†’            }.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
255â†’            // IDEA quirk:
256â†’            // if (true &&
257â†’            //     true
258â†’            // ) {
259â†’            // }
260â†’            // instead of
261â†’            // if (
262â†’            //     true &&
263â†’            //     true
264â†’            // ) {
265â†’            // }
266â†’            node.treeNext?.elementType != CONDITION
267â†’        ) {
268â†’            requireNewlineAfterLeaf(node, autoCorrect, emit)
269â†’        }
270â†’        if (!r.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
271â†’            requireNewlineBeforeLeaf(r, autoCorrect, emit, node.treeParent.indent())
272â†’        }
273â†’    }

275â†’    private fun rearrangeSuperTypeList(
276â†’        node: ASTNode,
277â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
278â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
279â†’    ) {
280â†’        val entries = (node.psi as KtSuperTypeList).entries
281â†’        if (
282â†’            node.textContains('\n') &&
283â†’            entries.size > 1 &&
284â†’            // e.g.
285â†’            //
286â†’            // class A : B, C,
287â†’            //     D
288â†’            // or
289â†’            // class A : B, C({
290â†’            // }), D
291â†’            //
292â†’            // but not
293â†’            //
294â†’            // class A : B, C, D({
295â†’            // })
296â†’            !(
297â†’                entries.dropLast(1).all { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_ENTRY } &&
298â†’                    entries.last().elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY
299â†’                )
300â†’        ) {
301â†’            // put space after :
302â†’            if (!node.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
303â†’                val colon = node.prevCodeLeaf()!!
304â†’                if (
305â†’                    !colon.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
306â†’                    colon.prevCodeLeaf().let { it?.elementType != RPAR || !it.prevLeaf().isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
307â†’                ) {
308â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(colon, autoCorrect, emit)
309â†’                }
310â†’            }
311â†’            // put entries on separate lines
312â†’            // TODO: group emit()s below with the one above into one (similar to ParameterListWrappingRule)
313â†’            for (c in node.children()) {
314â†’                if (c.elementType == COMMA &&
315â†’                    !c.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
316â†’                    !c.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine()
317â†’                ) {
318â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(
319â†’                        nodeAfterWhichNewlineIsRequired = c,
320â†’                        autoCorrect = autoCorrect,
321â†’                        emit = emit,
322â†’                        indent = node.indent(),
323â†’                    )
324â†’                }
325â†’            }
326â†’        }
327â†’    }

329â†’    private fun ASTNode.isFollowedByCommentOnSameLine() =
330â†’        nextLeaf { !it.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline() }
331â†’            ?.isPartOfComment() == true

333â†’    private fun rearrangeValueList(
334â†’        node: ASTNode,
335â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
336â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
337â†’    ) {
338â†’        for (c in node.children()) {
339â†’            val hasLineBreak =
340â†’                when (c.elementType) {
341â†’                    VALUE_ARGUMENT -> c.hasLineBreak(LAMBDA_EXPRESSION, FUN)
342â†’                    VALUE_PARAMETER, ANNOTATION -> c.hasLineBreak()
343â†’                    else -> false
344â†’                }
345â†’            if (hasLineBreak) {
346â†’                // rearrange
347â†’                //
348â†’                // a, b, value(
349â†’                // ), c, d
350â†’                //
351â†’                // to
352â†’                //
353â†’                // a, b,
354â†’                // value(
355â†’                // ),
356â†’                // c, d
357â†’
358â†’                // insert \n in front of multi-line value
359â†’                val prevSibling = c.prevSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
360â†’                if (
361â†’                    prevSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
362â†’                    !prevSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
363â†’                ) {
364â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(prevSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
365â†’                }
366â†’                // insert \n after multi-line value
367â†’                val nextSibling = c.nextSibling { it.elementType != WHITE_SPACE }
368â†’                val hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter =
369â†’                    c.isLastValueParameter() && c.firstChildNode.elementType == DESTRUCTURING_DECLARATION
370â†’                if (
371â†’                    nextSibling?.elementType == COMMA &&
372â†’                    !hasDestructuringDeclarationAsLastValueParameter &&
373â†’                    !nextSibling.treeNext.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() &&
374â†’                    // value(
375â†’                    // ), // a comment
376â†’                    // c, d
377â†’                    nextSibling.treeNext?.treeNext?.psi !is PsiComment
378â†’                ) {
379â†’                    requireNewlineAfterLeaf(nextSibling, autoCorrect, emit)
380â†’                }
381â†’            }
382â†’        }
383â†’    }

385â†’    private fun ASTNode.isLastValueParameter() =
386â†’        elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER &&
387â†’            siblings().none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }

389â†’    private fun rearrangeTypeArgumentList(
390â†’        node: ASTNode,
391â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
392â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
393â†’    ) {
394â†’        if (node.textContains('\n')) {
395â†’            // Each type projection must be preceded with a whitespace containing a newline
396â†’            node
397â†’                .children()
398â†’                .filter { it.elementType == TYPE_PROJECTION || it.elementType == TYPE_PARAMETER }
399â†’                .forEach { typeProjection ->
400â†’                    typeProjection
401â†’                        .prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
402â†’                        .let { prevSibling ->
403â†’                            if (prevSibling?.elementType == LT || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
404â†’                                emit(typeProjection.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${typeProjection.text}'", true)
405â†’                                if (autoCorrect) {
406â†’                                    typeProjection.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
407â†’                                        node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
408â†’                                    )
409â†’                                }
410â†’                            }
411â†’                        }
412â†’                }
413â†’
414â†’            // After the last type projection a whitespace containing a newline must exist
415â†’            node
416â†’                .findChildByType(GT)
417â†’                ?.let { closingAngle ->
418â†’                    val prevSibling = closingAngle.prevSibling { !it.isPartOfComment() }
419â†’                    if (prevSibling?.elementType != WHITE_SPACE || prevSibling.isWhiteSpaceWithoutNewline()) {
420â†’                        emit(closingAngle.startOffset, "A newline was expected before '${closingAngle.text}'", true)
421â†’                        if (autoCorrect) {
422â†’                            closingAngle.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(
423â†’                                node.treeParent.indent().plus(indentConfig.indent),
424â†’                            )
425â†’                        }
426â†’                    }
427â†’                }
428â†’
429â†’            Unit
430â†’        }
431â†’    }

433â†’    private fun rearrangeClosingQuote(
434â†’        node: ASTNode,
435â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
437â†’    ) {
438â†’        node
439â†’            .treeParent
440â†’            .takeIf { it.elementType == STRING_TEMPLATE }
441â†’            ?.let { it.psi as KtStringTemplateExpression }
442â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isMultiLine() }
443â†’            ?.takeIf { it.isFollowedByTrimIndent() || it.isFollowedByTrimMargin() }
444â†’            ?.takeIf { node.treePrev.text.isNotBlank() }
445â†’            ?.let {
446â†’                // rewriting
447â†’                // """
448â†’                //     text
449â†’                // _""".trimIndent()
450â†’                // to
451â†’                // """
452â†’                //     text
453â†’                // _
454â†’                // """.trimIndent()
455â†’                emit(
456â†’                    node.startOffset,
457â†’                    "Missing newline before \"\"\"",
458â†’                    true,
459â†’                )
460â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
461â†’                    node as LeafPsiElement
462â†’                    node.rawInsertBeforeMe(LeafPsiElement(LITERAL_STRING_TEMPLATE_ENTRY, "\n"))
463â†’                }
464â†’                LOGGER.trace { "$line: " + (if (!autoCorrect) "would have " else "") + "inserted newline before (closing) \"\"\"" }
465â†’            }

468â†’    private fun mustBeFollowedByNewline(node: ASTNode): Boolean {
469â†’        // find EOL token (last token before \n)
470â†’        // if token is in lTokenSet
471â†’        //     find matching rToken
472â†’        //     return true if there is no newline after the rToken
473â†’        // return false
474â†’        val nextCodeSibling = node.nextCodeSibling() // e.g. BINARY_EXPRESSION
475â†’        var lToken = nextCodeSibling?.nextLeaf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }?.prevCodeLeaf()
476â†’        if (lToken != null && lToken.elementType !in LTOKEN_SET) {
477â†’            // special cases:
478â†’            // x = y.f({ z ->
479â†’            // })
480â†’            // x = y.f(0, 1,

content.text:
I have implemented a fix to prevent `CommentWrappingRule` from wrapping block comments inside single-line blocks.

### Changes made:
- Modified `CommentWrappingRule.kt` to ignore block comments that are located inside a single-line block (specifically between `{` and `}` on the same line).
- Added helper method `isInsideSingleLineBlock()` to `CommentWrappingRule` to identify these cases.
- Added necessary imports for `LBRACE`, `RBRACE`, `nextCodeLeaf`, and `prevCodeLeaf`.

### Verification:
- Created a new test file `CommentWrappingRuleReproTest.kt` with test cases for:
    - Single-line lambda with a block comment: `val foo = { /* no-op */ }` (should be ignored).
    - Single-line `if` statement with block comments: `val foo = if (true) { /* no-op */ } else { /* no-op */ }` (should be ignored).
    - Block comment between other elements (not braces) on the same line: `val foo = /* comment */ "bar"` (should still be reported as disallowed).
- Verified that all new test cases pass.
- Verified that existing tests in `CommentWrappingRuleTest.kt` still pass.

```kotlin
// Example of code that is now kept unchanged:
val foo = { /* no-op */ }
```

 21â†’ * same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
 22â†’ */
 23â†’public class CommentWrappingRule :
 24â†’    StandardRule(
 25â†’        id = "comment-wrapping",
 26â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =

 36â†’    ) {
 37â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
 38â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment =
 39â†’                node
 40â†’                    .prevLeaf()
 41â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 42â†’            val nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment =
 43â†’                node
 44â†’                    .nextLeaf()
 45â†’                    ?.takeIf { isNonIndentLeafOnSameLine(it) }
 46â†’
 47â†’            if (nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment != null &&
 48â†’                nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment != null
 49â†’            ) {
 50â†’                if (hasNewLineInClosedRange(nonIndentLeafOnSameLinePrecedingBlockComment, nonIndentLeafOnSameLineFollowingBlockComment)) {
 51â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 52â†’                    //    val foo = "foo" /*
 53â†’                    //    some comment
 54â†’                    //    */ val bar = "bar"
 55â†’                    emit(
 56â†’                        node.startOffset,
 57â†’                        "A block comment starting on same line as another element and ending on another line before another element is " +
 58â†’                            "disallowed",
 59â†’                        false,
 60â†’                    )
 61â†’                } else {
 62â†’                    // Do not try to fix constructs like below:
 63â†’                    //    val foo /* some comment */ = "foo"
 64â†’                    emit(

 67â†’                        false,
 68â†’                    )

104â†’    }

107â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
108â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
109â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
110â†’        rawRemove()

114â†’        blockCommentNode: ASTNode,
115â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
116â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
117â†’    ) {
